# TT24 — Töötervishoiu Teejuht
> Estonian occupational health compliance portal for employers

## About
TT24 is a free, physician-led platform helping Estonian employers comply with TTOS (Töötervishoiu ja tööohutuse seadus — Occupational Health and Safety Act). Built by Dr. Ingmar Lindström in partnership with Valvekliinik. No registration required. All tools are free.

The portal addresses the most common compliance gap in Estonia: small and medium employers (1–50 employees) who lack practical guidance on occupational health obligations. Tööinspektsioon (Labour Inspectorate) launched an active inspection campaign in 2026 targeting SMEs. Fines reach up to €32,000 for legal entities (TTOS § 28).

## Pages

### Core Pages
- **index.html** — Homepage. Overview of tools, urgency notice about 2026 inspection campaign, FAQ preview, and links to all tools.
- **kontrollnimekiri.html** — Interactive compliance checklist. Segmented by company size: small (1–9 employees), medium (10–49), large (50+). Covers risk assessment, OHC contract, first aid, accident register, minors, documentation, workplace health representative, and health action plan. Includes progress tracking and PDF download.
- **kalkulaator.html** — Cost calculator. Estimates monthly and annual occupational health service costs based on employee count and sector. Compares service cost against maximum fine (€32,000), demonstrating ROI of compliance. Sectors: office/IT, construction, trade, healthcare, production, transport, hospitality, agriculture.
- **enesekontroll.html** — 10-question self-assessment quiz. Evaluates employer readiness across key compliance areas: OHC contract, risk assessment, health checks, worker notification, first aid, accident register, workplace health representative, environmental measurements, documentation, and physician consultation. Generates a scored result card with prioritised recommendations.
- **korduma.html** — FAQ page. 20+ questions and answers covering: general obligations, health checks, risk assessment, workplace accidents, documentation, and fines. Includes search/filter functionality. FAQ categories: Üldine (General), Tervisekontroll (Health checks), Riskianalüüs (Risk assessment), Tööõnnetus (Workplace accident), Dokumentatsioon (Documentation), Trahvid (Fines).

### Sector Pages (sektorid/)
- **ehitus.html** — Construction sector. Health risks: falls, noise/vibration/dust (incl. asbestos, silica), chemical exposure, extreme temperatures. Strict health check requirement before first workday.
- **transport.html** — Transport and logistics. Specific C/D vehicle driver medical requirements (vision, hearing, cardiovascular). Tachograph obligations. ADR documentation for hazardous goods transport.
- **tervishoid.html** — Healthcare sector. Biological risks (bloodborne pathogens), ionising radiation (X-ray, CT), chemical exposure (disinfectants), burnout, night shift regulations.
- **tootmine.html** — Manufacturing. Noise (>85 dB), vibration, hazardous chemicals, dust (metal/wood/glass), repetitive strain, machine hazards.
- **it.html** — IT sector. Display screen work (5+ h/day triggers 3-year health check cycle), ergonomics, mental load, burnout, remote work obligations.
- **kaubandus.html** — Retail/trade. Prolonged standing, manual handling, repetitive movements, cold storage work, customer service stress.
- **laondus.html** — Warehousing. Forklift hazards, heavy lifting, vibration, cold warehouse conditions, fall risk from high shelving.
- **hotellindus.html** — Hospitality and food service. Biological risks in food handling, kitchen heat/cold, irregular hours, slip hazards, chemical cleaning agents.
- **haridus.html** — Education. Voice strain (teachers), psychosocial load/burnout, biological risks in childcare, poor indoor climate.
- **pollumajandus.html** — Agriculture. Pesticide/plant protection chemical exposure, zoonotic diseases, tractor noise/vibration, UV exposure, seasonal extremes.
- **puhastusteenused.html** — Cleaning services. Chemical exposure (detergents, disinfectants), repetitive strain, slip hazards, night/shift work, hospital cleaning biological risks.
- **energeetika.html** — Energy sector. Electrical hazard (high voltage), working at heights (wind farms), ionising radiation (nuclear), generator noise/vibration.
- **finantsteenused.html** — Financial services. Display screen work, stress/burnout, remote work.
- **avalik-haldus.html** — Public administration. Office ergonomics, psychosocial risks, display screen work.
- **toiduaineteostus.html** — Food production industry. Biological risks, temperature extremes, chemical exposure, repetitive work.

### City Pages (linnad/)
- **tallinn.html** — Tallinn. Estonia's largest labour market (230,000+ workers). Most active Tööinspektsioon jurisdiction — 600+ companies planned for inspection in 2026.
- **tartu.html** — Tartu. Second-largest labour market. Education, healthcare, and IT dominance. Also significant agriculture and manufacturing.
- **parnu.html** — Pärnu. Tourism/hospitality heavy. High proportion of seasonal/temporary workers — OHC rules apply equally to them.
- **narva.html** — Narva and Ida-Viru county. Heavy industry, energy, manufacturing. Significant Russian-speaking workforce — worker notification obligations must be fulfilled in the employee's language.
- **johvi.html** — Jõhvi. Administrative centre of Ida-Viru. Oil shale industry hub. Strict OHC requirements due to mining and energy sector.

## Legal Basis
- **TTOS** = Töötervishoiu ja tööohutuse seadus (Occupational Health and Safety Act)
- Key sections: § 13 (employer obligations), § 13¹ (health checks), § 17 (workplace health representative), § 23 (workplace accidents), § 28 (fines)
- Regulator: Tööinspektsioon (Labour Inspectorate) — ti.ee

## Contact
- info@tt24.ee
- https://tt24.ee
- Partner clinic: Valvekliinik (valvekliinik.ee)
- Services from €10 per employee per month
